segments.text: Um, I won't go into it in any great detail,
but, um, well can, if you like.

has long complaints of the regulatory burden that they face.

amongst the operators.

and a thought that maybe the underlying objectives
of child safety and
and educational performance could be better, uh, achieved,
uh, with a simplified regime.
So the Ministry for Regulation, uh, carried out one
of its sector reviews in which it listened

parents, teachers, and so on.
Um, and it came up with 15 recommendations,
which the government accepted.
Um, this legislation is one part of that process.
Uh, part of it is changing the licensing criteria.
There's been quite an extensive, uh,
consultation carried out by the ministry, uh, on that.
That's, that's not in scope of this bill, except for some,
uh, rules around how future licensing criteria might be set.
Uh, it also, uh, will change who the regulator is,
uh, however that will be performed
as an education reform bill, as part
of the government's wider restructuring
of the education sector.

by Minister Stanford, uh, later this year.
Uh, she's currently working on that,
but it will also implement some of the recommendations
of this, uh, uh, early childhood sector review.
And then there is, uh, this part that we come to today, uh,
which is putting in place, uh, some statutory rules
around who is ultimately responsible for regulation of ECE
and what their procedures are, uh, for regulating.
And the purpose of that, um,
I think is probably best brought about by an experience
that an early childhood center had.

by its rural community, uh, went to the brink of shutdown,
uh, would've probably been shut down without some gentle
ministerial intervention.

a regulated party can tap on the sign

but that's, that's one of the big things
that has been a concern in the Australian context.

as some of the, um, things that seem

are there may actually impact very

And the other ones that the government wishes to get up

repeated comments about getting children ready for school.
The early childhood community does not consider
that early childhood is getting ready for school,
that we work with children in the here and now,
and that they are seen as competent and competent children.
And we are not looking at them in any, any sort
of future aspect.
Um, flexibility can sometimes be be used as a figure leaf
for lowering standards.
And, um, the saying
that regulation was burdensome without sufficient evidence
is what I think, um, has been discussed
this morning with the minister.
Mm-hmm. The institution,
the providers operating under relaxed
standards may behave in ways

and when competition is not strong.
Um, the, the thing, the things

They need to have resources to monitor risk

And I feel that this bill is very much a political

including teachers and parents
and children, which is why I recommend it doesn't, um,
continue to the second reading.
I'm going to show you a, um, a screen print that I've got.
This is a screen print of, um, an appeal by a woman
who was before the court.
Um, as an, as an owner
